summary: Tilting the face downward helps engage various muscles crucial for proper swallowing by activating those that protect against aspiration while ensuring correct passage through the upper digestive tract; it also aids in coordinating movements necessary throughout the entire process from start to finish. This position enhances overall safety and efficiency during swallows by optimizing muscle function and coordination essential for preventing choking hazards and maintaining appropriate pathways within the oral cavity leading towards either trachea or oesophagus depending on intended route taken post-swallow initiation triggered via voluntary actions initiated consciously under controlled conditions
